lead_signal.headline: EU Council adopts conclusions to counter Russia-linked hybrid threats including sabotage and FIMI.

autonomy_snapshot.rearm_europe_status: No new developments; prior EDF and PESCO projects stable
autonomy_snapshot.lead_signal: EU Council strengthens hybrid threat response amid Russian escalation signals.

domain_tracker.key_development: EU Council approves conclusions on hybrid threats; ECFR calls for offensive strategy
domain_tracker.policy_response: Enhanced hybrid toolbox, cyber diplomacy, critical infrastructure protection

defence_developments.headline: EU Council approves hybrid threat conclusions
defence_developments.summary: Council condemned Russia-linked activities including sabotage and FIMI. Adopted measures for prevention, deterrence via hybrid toolbox and cyber tools. Focus on infrastructure, elections, drones.

hybrid_threats.headline: Russian hybrid attacks in Europe quadrupled 2023-2024
hybrid_threats.summary: Study shows near quadrupling of attacks including 40 arson plots in Germany, Poland since 2018. Tactics cover assassinations, psychological warfare, arson. Escalation strains internal security.

institutional_developments.headline: EU Council conclusions on hybrid threats
institutional_developments.summary: Council adopted conclusions enhancing prevention, deterrence, response to hybrid campaigns. Integrates NIS2, Cyber Blueprint, ProtectEU. Targets sabotage, cyber, FIMI.

institutional_developments.headline: ECFR advocates offensive hybrid strategy
institutional_developments.summary: Publication urges shift from defensive to offensive actions in information, cyber, finance, kinetic domains. Highlights resilience failures amid sabotage, drones, disinformation.

us_eu_tracker.nato_status: Stable cooperation per 2023 Joint Declaration; no new tensions reported

lagrange_point_scores.key_metric: No new EDF or PESCO milestones
No AI or chips act progress

key_judgments.judgment: Russian hybrid attacks escalating, prompting EU institutional response but requiring offensive capabilities

weekly_brief_draft: Autonomy snapshot: Overall trajectory stable with lead movement in defence_security domain. Lead signal: EU Council adopts conclusions countering Russia-linked hybrid threats. Domain tracker shows defence_security stable at preliminary score 0.4 amid high RU dependency, with policy response via hybrid toolbox. Other domains stable with no new developments. US-EU tracker aligned, NATO status stable per ongoing cooperation frameworks. Hybrid threats feature Russian escalation with attacks quadrupling 2023-2024, including arson plots; confidence probable. Defence developments centre on EU Council measures targeting sabotage, FIMI. No member state tracker updates or election threats within 90 days. Lagrange points incremental in diplomatic capability via Council action, stalled elsewhere. Key judgment: Escalating Russian hybrids demand offensive EU strategy. No ReArm Europe, EDIP, EDF advances; prior status carried forward. Institutional developments include ECFR offensive strategy call, aligning with Council conclusions. Trajectory reflects institutional rhetoric outpacing capability, per four-actor framework emphasizing RU pressure.

domains.headline: Hybrid threat response strengthens but RU escalation persists

cross_monitor_candidates.signal: Russian hybrid escalation includes FIMI components
